2606.23107.title: A Dual-Track Framework for Template-Constrained LaTeX Conversion
2606.23107.paper_summary: With the increasing demands for advanced document conversion, mapping structured Markdown drafts into template-compliant formats like LaTeX remains a challenge. Existing approaches largely depend on either deterministic rule-based converters or pure end-to-end Large Language Model (LLM) generation. The former fails to correctly handle asset insertions and template-specific constraints, while the latter tends to induce semantic drift, leading to hallucinations that are difficult to debug. To address these limitations, we introduce a robust Dual-Track Framework that systematically decouples template formatting from document processing: an offline track extracts template constraints into a reusable manifest, while an online track implements a hybrid execution pipeline. This pipeline confines LLM usage exclusively to reasoning-intensive components (e.g., semantic metadata, bibliographic references, and complex visual/tabular layouts) while delegating rule-based engines for deterministic processing. Empirical evaluation across 7 LaTeX templates and 56 published research papers demonstrates that our method preserves better structural fidelity, satisfies diverse layout constraints, and achieves a higher compilation success rate compared to the previous baselines.
2606.23107.paper_summary_zh: 隨著對高級文件轉換需求的增加，將結構化的Markdown草稿映射到符合模板的格式（如LaTeX）仍然是一個挑戰。現有的方法主要依賴於確定性基於規則的轉換器或純端到端的大型語言模型（LLM）生成。前者無法正確處理資產插入和模板特定的約束，而後者則往往會導致語義漂移，產生難以調試的幻覺。為了解決這些限制，我們提出了一個穩健的雙軌框架，系統性地將模板格式化與文件處理解耦：離線軌道將模板約束提取到可重用的清單中，而在線軌道則實現了一個混合執行管道。該管道將LLM的使用限制在推理密集型組件（例如，語義元數據、文獻引用和複雜的視覺/表格佈局）上，同時將確定性處理委派給基於規則的引擎。對7個LaTeX模板和56篇已發表研究論文的實證評估表明，我們的方法在結構保真度方面表現更佳，滿足多樣的佈局約束，並且相比於之前的基準達到了更高的編譯成功率。
2606.23107.author: Chung Cheuk Hei et.al.
